proposed_levers.lever: Arm-when-engaged: delete the SE entry-gap gate on the funded endpoint geometry
proposed_levers.mechanism: endpoint_recovery.py:90-92 arms the ledger-funded arm (LR capped at endpoint_floor_lr 2e-3, cosine over budget) only when (target-entry) >= accuracy_se; otherwise the stage runs the unarmed patience branch that its own docstring calls sterile (endpoint_recovery.py:69-70) — t0_14 measured exactly +0.0000 for 210 steps at LR 3e-3. Fix: whenever engaged (entry<target) and the endpoint_steps ledger affords steps, use the armed geometry. The target-reach early stop (basic_trainer_steps.py:157-171, reach check precedes the min_steps guard) bounds spend: t0_11 covered a 1.22pp gap in 422 steps (~12s).
proposed_levers.code_seams: src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/frontier/endpoint_recovery.py:90-92 and 104 (the `if floor_lifted or entry_gap_armed` condition); keep-best + fp32 entry guard + rollback (lines 133-140) already make it non-destructive.
proposed_levers.config_keys: none new; reads existing endpoint_floor_steps / endpoint_floor_lr (tuning_policy.py:61,68)
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: t0_14: +0.3-0.7pp (0.9597 -> ~0.962-0.966, entry->highwater gap 0.66pp) at +10-30s wall (~400 steps at 37.9 steps/s). Also removes the razor-edge arming nondeterminism (t0_11 armed by 0.0002).
proposed_levers.generic: Mode- and vehicle-generic: removes a threshold whose scale (accuracy_se) is itself a vehicle/eval-batch artifact; no constants added; applies to every conversion family's WQ endpoint identically.
proposed_levers.risk: Slightly more endpoint training on cells that previously took the sterile branch; bounded by the shared run ledger and early stop.

proposed_levers.lever: Convergence-stop inside the armed geometry (replaces min_steps=budget)
proposed_levers.mechanism: endpoint_recovery.py:116 sets min_steps=budget, disarming patience for the WHOLE funded budget, so an unreachable floor burns everything (t0_09: 2000/2000 for +0.21pp; a 16k grant would burn ~208-450s/cell at the measured 35.5-77.1 steps/s). Fix: keep the cosine spanning the full funded budget but re-enable keep-best stagnation patience at a coarse scale (e.g. N check-intervals without min_improvement), so an unreachable floor stops ~patience*interval past the last improvement while a live climb (t0_09's 0.9746 read, t01_06's 0.9595) keeps its funding. This is the cheaper generic lever the floor grant needs, and it is what lets generate.py:521's 2000 cap be deleted — that cap's own comment admits 'the cap was the de-facto budget' precisely because floor-lifted stages cannot stop early today.
proposed_levers.code_seams: src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/frontier/endpoint_recovery.py:116 (min_steps), src/mimarsinan/model_training/basic_trainer_steps.py:173-189 (existing patience machinery is reused, not new); then test_configs/generate.py:508-521 drops the per-family 2000 override.
proposed_levers.config_keys: wq_endpoint_recovery_steps returns to the recipe values (600 base / 16000 well-conditioned); endpoint_floor_steps=16000 ledger unchanged
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: t01_06: +0.5-1.0pp (0.9536 -> ~0.958-0.963, finishing the AQ-crater recovery it was annealed out of) at +25-100s stagnation-stopped. t0_09: +0.4-0.8pp (0.9686 -> ~0.972-0.975; its own trajectory touched 0.9746, and the 5u probe precedent is 0.9663->0.9761 at 16k, conversion_policy.py:26-27) at +100-200s instead of the naive +208s full burn. Worst-case wall on saturated cells collapses from ~16000 steps to ~last-improvement + patience*42.
proposed_levers.generic: Pure geometry change at the single shared endpoint seam; no mode, vehicle, or dataset constants; the same code serves lif/sync/cascaded/ttfs/ttfsq endpoints.
proposed_levers.risk: A stagnation window too small could stop a slow climb early; mitigate by scaling patience with budget/check_interval (still constant-free).

diagnoses.surprises: 1) The three ttfsq runs have THREE different binders (reached / budget-annealed-out / never-armed) — the group's 'consistent small deficit' is an aggregate of distinct mechanisms, so no single budget number fixes all; the minimal fix must be the geometry (arm-when-engaged + convergence-stop), after which the floor grant and cap deletion become one-liners. 2) t0_11 armed by 0.0002 (gap 0.00940 vs SE 0.00922) — the SE arming gate is a coin flip on healthy cells; t0_14 lost that flip and got exactly +0.0000 from its endpoint. 3) The sub-SE join condition that spiking_semantics.py:76-78 demands before ttfsq may carry the floor is already satisfied by these runs' own measurements (SCM deltas <=0.14pp), i.e. the evidence to promote ttfsq was produced by the runs being diagnosed. 4) generate.py's cap rationale ('non-mixer families cannot reach by grinding') is contradicted on t0_09: its endpoint hit 0.9746 progress reads ABOVE its pruned 0.9654 envelope before the 2000-step cosine annealed it out — grinding was paying when the cap stopped it. 5) The A4 exact-kernel hypothesis is measured second-order here: the ttfsq proxy is already deploy-faithful to <=0.14pp on all three cells. 6) ~2000 of t0_09's 2330 log lines are per-step [BiasSaturation] prints from the endpoint's QAT reprojection — harmless but it hides the trajectory and deserves throttling.

proposed_levers.risk: Staircase-warm-start POISON verdict does NOT apply (this is in-the-loop backward hedging, never staircase weights; the refuted lever was pure-staircase pretraining). Residual risk: cells where genuine and staircase logits decorrelate (mmixcore post-collapse corr ~0.0) get a gradient aimed at the staircase optimum — mitigated by mix=0.5 + the existing keep-best rung restore and the gated ladder's reject path. Default-off = byte-identical pipelines.
proposed_levers.generic: Backward-only, no dataset/vehicle constants; applies to any cascaded-TTFS vehicle at any S/depth because it only requires the already-universal pair (installed genuine forward with _unpatched_forward, TTFSActivation nodes). Tier1/2 scaling: cost is one extra analytical forward per training step (cheap vs the O(S x depth) genuine walk); the flag rides the existing ConversionPolicy recipe door so tier matrices pick it up by regenerating test_configs via generate.py.

proposed_levers.lever: L2: thread the hedged STE through BOTH P1'' endpoints (FT endpoint + WQ endpoint recovery), config ttfs_endpoint_staircase_ste (or fold into ttfs_staircase_ste)
proposed_levers.mechanism: Endpoint recovery currently trains tuner.trainer.loss_function (plain CE / KD) through the installed genuine-cascade forward, and it is measurably DESTRUCTIVE (lenet5 WQ 0.668->0.09 over 2000 steps, bbce29c3; mmixcore FT 0.47->0.24 over 920 steps, ea6a5382:248-287; simplemlp WQ 0.52->0.12, 52955127:258-1276) — keep-best clamps exit=entry, so 2000 funded steps and the lifted 0.98 floor are burned for nothing on every collapsed cell. Swapping the recovery-window loss for the hedged STE turns the largest existing step budget in the pipeline into usable training.
proposed_levers.code_seams: run_endpoint_recovery, src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/frontier/endpoint_recovery.py:98-132 — add an optional loss_override swapped onto tuner.trainer.loss_function around RecoveryEngine.train_to_target (engine entry src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/recovery_engine.py:17; loss consumed at src/mimarsinan/model_training/basic_trainer.py:221). Call sites that pass it: TTFSCycleAdaptationTuner._post_stabilization_hook, src/mimarsinan/tuning/tuners/ttfs_cycle_adaptation_tuner.py:530-535, and NormalizationAwarePerceptronQuantizationTuner._post_stabilization_hook, src/mimarsinan/tuning/tuners/normalization_aware_perceptron_quantization_tuner.py:69-110. Mode predicate for the WQ tuner (which is family-agnostic): SpikingDeploymentContract.training_forward_kind() == "segment_spike" (the SSOT predicate already used at ttfs_cycle_adaptation_tuner.py:114-119) — never a mode-string ladder.
proposed_levers.config_keys: ttfs_staircase_ste gates it; optionally ttfs_endpoint_staircase_ste for independent ablation
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: lenet5 s16/s32: the ONLY binder — WQ entry 0.62-0.67 with 2000 steps that currently anti-train; with a usable gradient plus the already-armed 0.98 floor funding, predict exit >=0.83-0.90 -> both retention trips clear (deepcnn recovers +0.8pp through the SAME seam when its gradient works, and its projection crater is the shallow analogue). deepmlp/simplemlp frozen WQ endpoints (0.767/0.770/0.833/0.870 entry=exit): predict +5-15pp each, finals 0.77-0.87 -> 0.85-0.93. mmixcore: endpoint entry is post-collapse (~0.5); STE recovery alone predicted to reach 0.7-0.85 (toy revive-then-refine analogue), retention pass marginal without L1 — L1+L2 together is the bet.
proposed_levers.risk: Same as L1 plus: the WQ endpoint trains the float aux model and reprojects (normalization_aware...py:71-73) — the STE staircase branch must be evaluated on the SAME reprojection-wrapped model the trainer steps, otherwise the hedge is stale; keep-best + fp32 entry guard + rollback (endpoint_recovery.py:136-140) already bound the downside to exit=entry (today's behavior).
proposed_levers.generic: endpoint_recovery.py is the generic P1'' engine shared by every conversion family (LIF/sync/cascaded/WQ) — the override is an opt-in parameter, inert for every non-cascaded family; no constants; scales to tier1/2 unchanged since budgets stay STEP-denominated (endpoint_floor_steps ledger).

proposed_levers.lever: L3: split-LR optimizer groups — theta (activation_scale) at theta_lr_ratio x the weight LR (config: enable existing ttfs_theta_cotrain + new ttfs_theta_lr_ratio, policy default 25.0 = 5e-2/2e-3)
proposed_levers.mechanism: The proven toy recipe is w=2e-3 / theta=5e-2 split LRs: theta (firing gain) must move an order of magnitude faster than weights to revive depth-attenuated firing (dead-neuron theta-calib has no gradient at the pipeline LR; the runs show dead_fraction 4.4-6.2 on mmixcore, ea6a5382:227). Today _ensure_fast_optimizer builds ONE group at min(pipeline_lr, prefix_stage_lr=1e-3) (fast_ladder.py:150, ttfs_cycle_adaptation_tuner.py:353-362, tuning_policy.py:50) and endpoint recovery runs one group at endpoint_floor_lr=2e-3 (tuning_policy.py:61) — theta is either frozen (requires_grad=False, ttfs_spiking.py:66-69) or crawls at the weight LR. Ratio is expressed relative to the resolved stage LR, not as an absolute — no vehicle constants.
proposed_levers.code_seams: Param partition source: _promote_per_channel_theta populates self._theta_cotrain_params, src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/kd_blend_adaptation_tuner.py:118-132 (gated by ttfs_theta_cotrain, src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/calibration_pipeline.py:132; per-channel theta is already WQ-safe per src/mimarsinan/transformations/perceptron/perceptron_transformer.py:96). Optimizer seams: _ensure_fast_optimizer, src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/fast_ladder.py:143-162 (build two param groups: theta ids at lr*ratio, rest at lr — precedent for grouped builds: build_param_groups, src/mimarsinan/model_training/training_recipe.py:88-137); endpoint path: build the grouped optimizer in run_endpoint_recovery and pass it via the EXISTING optimizer= parameter of RecoveryEngine.train_to_target (src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/recovery_engine.py:17, default build at :49). Ratio constant lives in TuningPolicy (src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/tuning_policy.py:19-68) as theta_lr_ratio.
proposed_levers.config_keys: ttfs_theta_cotrain (exists, default false), ttfs_theta_lr_ratio (float, policy default 25.0)
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: The STE integration plan measured the single shared LR as the suspected cause of the 0.82 MNIST cap vs 0.966 toy with split LRs — so L3 is the unlock multiplier on L1/L2 rather than standalone: predict L1+L3 closes the remaining ~1-2pp between 'rung accepted' and 'target reached' on deepmlp/mmixcore, and stabilizes the within-rung keep-best oscillation. Standalone (without L1): small or nil — gradient theta-calib through the fire-once surrogate was REFUTED (dead-neuron no-gradient), which is exactly why the runs did not enable it.
proposed_levers.risk: theta drift can violate downstream quantization-grid assumptions if WQ reads a stale theta — mitigated: per-channel theta is threaded through the perceptron transformer (perceptron_transformer.py:96) and the WQ projection re-reads live scales; keep default-off and land behind the same recipe door.
proposed_levers.generic: Pure optimizer-group mechanics on parameters that every cascaded vehicle has; ratio-relative (multiplies whatever LR the stage resolved), so it transfers across workload LR profiles; LIF already has the sibling flag (lif_theta_cotrain, lif_adaptation_plan.py:66) so the seam is family-symmetric.

proposed_levers.lever: L4 (small, gate-correctness): deduplicate hop-frontier refinement retries that map to the same k
proposed_levers.mechanism: On a discrete frontier of n hops, [MBH-GATE] midpoint refinement halves the RATE (0.111->0.0556->0.0278->0.0139) but every retry still maps to k=1, re-training and re-probing an identical state: b6d954ba:235-243 and 46c8171b:234-242 show four bit-identical full_acc reads (0.900070 / 0.889276). Snap retry rates to the frontier grid (multiples of 1/n) and stop when the snapped k repeats.
proposed_levers.code_seams: Retry-rate computation in gated_fast_rate_attempt, src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/mbh_gate.py (the reject/retry_rate emitter seen in every [MBH-GATE] reject line); rate->k mapping owned by PrefixConversionAxis, src/mimarsinan/tuning/axes/blend_axis.py, and PrefixGenuineForward.frontier_k (src/mimarsinan/models/spiking/training/prefix_genuine_forward.py).
proposed_levers.config_keys: none (behavior fix under the existing gate; a TuningPolicy bool if we want it staged)
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: No accuracy claim; reclaims 3 x hop_stage_steps_per_rate=40 wasted training steps + 3 full-transform probes per stalled rung (every mmixcore run) — that freed budget flows straight into the endpoint via freed_ladder_steps (endpoint_recovery.py:35-44), where L2 makes it useful.
proposed_levers.risk: Minimal; changes only duplicate-measurement behavior on discrete axes. Must keep continuous-axis (blend) refinement untouched.
proposed_levers.generic: Pure gate-geometry fix, axis-type-driven, no constants; benefits any discrete-frontier tuner (also the sync hop-staged install, conversion_policy.py:87).

diagnoses.surprises: 1) The 'WQ endpoint NO-OP' premise is WRONG in an important way: it is not an inert optimizer — the 2000 recovery steps train the deployed cascade to CHANCE (lenet5 0.668->0.641->0.29->0.09 stuck at 0.09, bbce29c3:306-2447; simplemlp 0.52->0.12, 52955127:258-1276; mmixcore FT endpoint 0.47->0.24, ea6a5382:248-287) and keep-best clamps exit=entry. entry==exit is the destructive-gradient signature, which makes the STE levers HIGHER-leverage than a dead-gradient reading would (the budget, floor arming, and engine all already work — only the gradient direction is wrong). 2) TASK-3 CONFIRMED with a nuance: the genuine backward is the fire-once surrogate — per-cycle ATan Heaviside (src/mimarsinan/models/nn/activations/ttfs_spiking.py:13-17 -> _StrictHeavisideFunction, src/mimarsinan/models/nn/activations/lif.py:51-67) masked by (1.0 - self._has_fired) with has_fired accumulating spike.detach() (ttfs_spiking.py:153-155 encoding, :180-182 general) — gradient exists ONLY at the single fire cycle; driven per-cycle by the cascade walk n.forward at src/mimarsinan/spiking/segment_policy_ttfs.py:265 (cycle_accurate set in prepare, :36-41). No staircase-STE exists anywhere on the current branch (grep 'staircase_ste|StaircaseSte|hedged' over src/ = 0 hits; _StaircaseSteKDLoss lives only on campaign-ws1-capabilities). Additionally ALL 12 runs ran with ttfs_theta_cotrain/ttfs_boundary_surrogate/ttfs_gain_correction OFF (zero log hits), so offload-boundary gradients were fully SEVERED too (hard train returned at segment_policy_ttfs.py:180-182 when temp is None). 3) The repo working tree is on branch 'main' at 356e228f, not the 'monotone-adaptation' the session snapshot claimed — all file:line citations above are against main. 4) Gate-refinement waste on discrete frontiers: 4 identical probe reads per stalled hop rung (0.900070 x4). 5) deepcnn d8 S=4 is a clean control: the SAME endpoint engine climbs (FT 0.9669->0.9819, WQ 0.9805->0.9885, 350cfe61:272,2341) — destructiveness is depth/S-conditioned exactly as the d_max(S)~0.56*sqrt(S) law predicts, so any fix must be measured for no-regression there. 6) Draw variance is concentrated in the genuine k-hybrid reads, not the ANN side: identical deepmlp d16 configs differ by up to 29pp at the same rung (0.2754 vs 0.5682 at k=3/8), and a single rung's training oscillates 0.146<->0.813 between keep-best probes — the fire-once gradient is not just weak but chaotic, which is why the group reads 0.46-0.96 across draws.

proposed_levers.lever: Plateau-adaptive floor: drop min_steps==budget, stop on SE-flat, refund the ledger
proposed_levers.mechanism: run_endpoint_recovery sets min_steps=budget for ledger-funded stages (frontier/endpoint_recovery.py:116), which disarms the patience branch in train_steps_until_target (basic_trainer_steps.py:179 'step_idx+1 >= min_s') for the entire 16k — every armed floor burns its full budget even when flat after 8k (t01_07) or dead at chance (t01_05, t0_21). Replace with: min_steps = a small multiple of patience*check_interval (keeps the documented '210-step window is sterile inside the lr dip' arming rationale, endpoint_recovery.py:69), patience widened to O(20-40) checks with min_improvement=accuracy_se (already threaded at endpoint_recovery.py:128), and unconsumed steps refunded to the run ledger (endpoint_steps.consume already records exact steps_used, endpoint_recovery.py:133-134).
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: -150..-250s wall per floor cell (measured floor wall 389-466s, ~25-29s/1k steps; 8k-16k pays 0-0.1pp median) at <=0.1-0.4pp accuracy cost; converts the unreachable 0.98 floor (envelope ~0.97, so reached=False always) from a fixed 16k wall into climb-until-SE-flat
proposed_levers.generic: Pure engine geometry in units of checks and accuracy_se — no dataset, vehicle, or mode constant; applies identically to every armed endpoint on tier-1/2 vehicles where each step is far more expensive
proposed_levers.risk: Truncated cosine never anneals fully; mitigated by keep-best restore (already entry-anchored) or by triggering the existing plateau-LR rebuild (basic_trainer_steps.py:70-89) instead of a hard stop

proposed_levers.lever: Divergence guard + warm-up + LR-backoff rescue for the floor arm
proposed_levers.mechanism: The floor enters at lr 2e-3 stone-cold: train_to_target is called with no warmup (endpoint_recovery.py:118-132; warmup_steps param exists at basic_trainer_steps.py:97; tuning_recipe warmup_ratio=0 per run headers). 2/8 floors collapsed to chance within <=84 steps and trained dead for 16k steps (t01_05 first check 0.098; t0_21 0.9205 then 0.10). Guard: if best has not beaten entry-SE after N checks AND current val < pipeline_hard_floor (already computed in BUDGET, e.g. 0.811), restore best and restart the remaining budget at lr*recovery_lr_plateau_factor with a 1-2% warmup. The plateau machinery ALREADY EXISTS default-off (TuningPolicy.recovery_lr_plateau=False, factor 0.3, reductions 2, tuning_policy.py:28-30; plateau_lr_factor/reductions params at basic_trainer_steps.py:105-106,180-188) — endpoint_recovery simply never passes it.
proposed_levers.config_keys: TuningPolicy.recovery_lr_plateau / recovery_lr_plateau_factor / recovery_lr_plateau_reductions; endpoint_floor_lr (workload-overridable via ResolvedWorkloadProfile, tuning_policy.py:86-90); tuning_recipe.warmup_ratio
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: +3.5pp on t0_21-class cells (measured identical-config sibling t01_24: 0.9671 vs 0.9316) and +2-4pp on t01_05-class; zero change on healthy floors (guard only fires below entry-SE); no added wall — reuses the same step budget
proposed_levers.generic: Divergence is detected relative to entry and pipeline_hard_floor (both run-derived), backoff is a multiplicative factor — mode/vehicle-agnostic; fragile-at-entry genuine forwards (sync S small, deep cascades) exist across the whole tier-1/2 matrix
proposed_levers.risk: Low: keep-best + fp32 entry guard already make the stage non-destructive; worst case the backoff also diverges and the cell lands where it lands today

proposed_levers.lever: Entitle ttfs_quantized (and any reached=True cell) to the well-conditioned WQ floor
proposed_levers.mechanism: _TTFS_QUANTIZED_RECIPE_KNOBS carries no floor (conversion_policy.py:205-209) unlike lif/sync/cascaded which append _WELL_CONDITIONED_ENDPOINT_FLOOR_KNOBS (conversion_policy.py:65,88,106), and ttfs_quantized is explicitly outside is_bit_parity_lossless_conversion (spiking_semantics.py:73-82: 'near-lossless modes ... join only after that gap is verified sub-SE'). Measured consequence: t0_11 STOPPED at the D-hat highwater target 0.9596 with 1578/2000 steps unspent (reached=True) — the TARGET, not capacity, was the binder; t01_06 exhausted 2000 still climbing +0.9pp/1k. The mixer NF<->SCM WQ-residual verdict (memory: honest sub-SE rounding) satisfies the stated join condition. Add wq_endpoint_target_floor 0.98 + the ledger-funded budget to the ttfsq row, scoped to the final WQ endpoint exactly like sync/lif (normalization_aware_perceptron_quantization_tuner.py:76-89 takes max of endpoint_target_floor/wq_endpoint_target_floor).
proposed_levers.code_seams: src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/conversion_policy.py:67-71 (add knobs to _TTFS_QUANTIZED_RECIPE_KNOBS); src/mimarsinan/tuning/tuners/normalization_aware_perceptron_quantization_tuner.py:76-89 (no change needed); src/mimarsinan/chip_simulation/spiking_semantics.py:73-82 (audit predicate note)
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: t01_06 0.9536 -> ~0.962-0.965, t0_11 0.9637 -> ~0.968 (same-family t01_23 climbed +2.3pp from entry 0.9467 toward the ~0.97 asymptote); with lever 1's adaptive stop the cost is +100-200s, not +400s
proposed_levers.generic: A mode-recipe row in the SSOT table gated by the existing sub-SE-parity join condition — no vehicle constants; every ttfsq cell in tiers 0-2 inherits it
proposed_levers.risk: Wall growth if deployed without lever 1 (a full 16k floor adds ~380s/cell); the join condition must stay predicate-audited so lossy modes don't inherit the floor

proposed_levers.lever: Marginal-slope ledger allocation: let starved post-crater endpoints (600/2000-step caps) draw from the run's endpoint-step ledger
proposed_levers.mechanism: The run already owns ONE step ledger (endpoint_steps.py) but run_endpoint_recovery only uses it to CLAMP armed stages (endpoint_recovery.py:112-116, budget=min(budget, steps_left)) — it never raises the fixed base budgets (endpoint_recovery_steps=600 at conversion_policy.py:53/77/104; wq 2000 in the lif/ttfsq headers). Measured mis-allocation: t01_21's LIF endpoint was cut at +13.6pp/1k slope after 600 steps and its WQ endpoint at +3.7pp/1k after 2000, while floor tails elsewhere paid 0-0.1pp/1k for the same steps. Change: an entry-gap-armed stage may draw budget beyond base up to the ledger remainder, continuing while the keep-best slope over the last patience window exceeds accuracy_se (identical stop rule to lever 1) — run-total steps unchanged, just re-allocated to the steepest stage.
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: LIF S=4 cells +2-4pp (t0_01 0.9078 and t01_08 0.9386 were climbing 2.0-2.7pp/1k at their 2000-step cutoffs; t01_21 0.9431 at 3.7pp/1k) for +50-100s each, funded by lever-1 savings; sync S=4 AQ endpoints (entries 0.63-0.76 recovering at 600-step cutoff) similarly
proposed_levers.generic: Allocation is by measured slope in SE-per-check units against a shared step ledger — no mode, dataset, or S constants; exactly the deployed-S allocation gap the frontier program flagged (R2 REFINE)
proposed_levers.risk: A stage with a noisy val window could over-draw; bound each draw per stage (e.g. <=50% of remainder) and keep the fp32 entry guard

proposed_levers.lever: Raise the float envelope within the epoch budget: keep-best pretraining + recipe knobs already plumbed
proposed_levers.mechanism: Envelope-bound cells end AT their pretrain read (t01_06 0.9536 vs 0.9540; t01_02 0.9609 vs 0.9630) and pretrain reads scatter 0.9540-0.9703 across the SAME architecture — ~1.6pp of schedule/seed variance. Pretraining commits LAST-epoch weights: train_until_target_accuracy has no keep-best snapshot (basic_trainer_epochs.py:31-55), unlike the step engine's entry-anchored keep-best (basic_trainer_steps.py:141-143). Pretrain costs 48-68s vs the floor's 389-466s. Changes, all inside the existing epoch budget: (a) per-epoch keep-best with a full-val confirm read (mirror the _TARGET_CONFIRM_FACTOR pattern, basic_trainer_steps.py:14-17); (b) exercise the recipe knobs that exist but sit at zero in every header — training_recipe.label_smoothing (training_recipe.py:40), warmup_ratio (0.05 now; sweep 0.02-0.1); (c) where the cell spec allows epochs, e2->e4 measured +0.6-1.4pp for +~30s (t01_06 0.9540 e2 vs e4 siblings 0.9604-0.9703).
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: +0.3-1.0pp on envelope-bound cells (ttfsq, lif s16, analytical ttfs with strong pretrain) for <=+30s — cheapest pp/s in the whole pipeline; also lifts every floor's asymptote since the floor saturates at the envelope
proposed_levers.generic: training_recipe is the SSOT for all vehicles/datasets; keep-best-epochs is a trainer-engine property with no constants; directly transferable to tier-1/2 where pretrain variance is larger
proposed_levers.risk: Keep-best on small val windows selects noise — requires the confirm-read pattern; label smoothing was previously kept default-off (D4), so gate it per-recipe and ablate

diagnoses.surprises: (1) The two 'zero-gain' floors did not diminish — they DIED: t01_05 trained at chance (0.098-0.11) from its first check ~30 steps in, and t0_21 collapsed from 0.9205 to 0.10 right after its first check; both then burned the full 16,000 steps (~440-460s GPU) dead because min_steps==budget disarms every stop (endpoint_recovery.py:116), and the entry-anchored keep-best silently restored entry so the ledger shows only exit==entry to 6 decimals. t0_21's 0.9316 verdict-row acc is therefore an artifact — its identical-config sibling t01_24 read 0.9671 (a 3.5pp coin-flip on floor divergence). (2) t01_23's deployed final (0.9694) EXCEEDS its own float pretrain (0.9580) by +1.1pp — the floor is genuine QAT that trains through the pretrain read, so the binder is the ~0.97 fbu asymptote (tuning_policy.py:66), not the pretrain number itself. (3) The 16k floor equals ~34 MNIST epochs of data — 8-17x the pretraining phase (2-4 epochs) — making it by far the largest training phase in the pipeline while paying 0-0.1pp/1k over its second half. (4) t0_11 exposed a target-ceiling pathology: reached=True STOPPED it at the D-hat highwater (0.9596) with 79% of budget unspent, purely because ttfs_quantized is denied the 0.98 floor by the recipe table — the mode's conversion is the cleanest in the group. (5) The LIF cells ran floor-LIFTED (wq_endpoint_target_floor 0.98 in headers) but with wq_endpoint_recovery_steps pinned at 2000, contradicting the recipe SSOT's 16000 (conversion_policy.py:41-44,65) — the campaign config starved exactly the cells with the steepest cutoff slopes (up to +13.6pp/1k). (6) All rescue machinery for divergence (plateau LR backoff, warmup_steps parameter) already exists in the codebase default-off/unpassed — no new mechanism is needed, only wiring at the endpoint_recovery call site.

Cost-class totals (group = 14,676s): WQ-endpoint floor burn = 5,980s (41%), of which zero/noise-gain (< 1x accuracy_se) = 2,797s (19%: t0_21 456.1, t01_05 436.5, t01_07 390.5, t0_19 315.3, t01_11 309.1, t0_04 287.0, t0_17 ~250, t01_02 193.3, t01_14 159.1); armed ladder+LR-find+gate evals ~2,400s (16%); sim+mapping ~2,540s (17%: Loihi 890, nevresim Simulation 603, HCM/SCM ~720, deep_cnn ModelBuilding 150, misc); mode endpoints (FT/LIF/AQ) ~800s (5.5%); pretraining ~1,410s (10%); pruning 357s; remainder = small steps. Mechanism proof: endpoint_recovery.py:104-117 sets min_steps=budget when floor_lifted OR entry_gap_armed; basic_trainer_steps.py:179 gates the patience stop on `step_idx + 1 >= min_s`, so patience NEVER fires; the only exit left is a confirmed target-reach (basic_trainer_steps.py:157-171) and the 0.98 floor target (conversion_policy.py:29,42) is unreachable on these cells -> every armed endpoint logs steps_used == budget, reached=False (all 24 armed MBH-ENDPOINT lines). Unarmed endpoints stop at patience*check_interval = 5*42 = 210 steps (tuner_base.py:28, e.g. t01_19 LIF ep 210/600, WQ 252/2000). Eval cadence: BUDGET lines show check_interval=42, progress_eval_batches=16 (2048 samples/check), eval_n_batches=23 (2944 samples, accuracy_se=0.00922) -> per 42-step window the loop trains 1344 samples (fwd+bwd) and evals 2048 (fwd) through the SAME armed forward, ~25-35% of armed-stage wall. Armed step rate is mode/S-dependent: analytic ttfs 43.4 steps/s, sync ~35-38, LIF S=4 20-27, LIF S=16 10.3, LIF S=32 7.0, genuine cascade d16 6.5 -> the SAME 2000-step floor costs 50s (analytic) to 315s (deep cascade).

proposed_levers.lever: Convergence-stop for min_steps=budget endpoint/stabilize stages (keep-best patience with budget-scaled horizon)
proposed_levers.mechanism: Replace min_steps=budget with min_steps = LR-dip cover (a budget FRACTION, e.g. 20% of the cosine, generalizing the measured ~1.6k/16k sterile dip) and re-arm patience in units of checks-since-last-new-best scaled to budget (e.g. no new best >= min_improvement for 25% of budget => stop). Keep-best already anchors at entry and restores best (basic_trainer_steps.py:141-145,191-192) and the outer entry guard rolls back (endpoint_recovery.py:136-140), so ANY stop point is non-destructive; stop stays step-deterministic, preserving the reproducibility contract (endpoint_recovery.py:75-77).
proposed_levers.code_seams: src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/frontier/endpoint_recovery.py:104-117 (min_steps=budget at :116); src/mimarsinan/model_training/basic_trainer_steps.py:179 (patience gate, already supports it); src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/smooth_adaptation_run.py:109 (_stabilize_at_full_rate min_steps=budget - the t01_06 AQ seam); src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/tuning_policy.py:58-68 (TUNING_POLICY new endpoint_floor_min_fraction/patience_fraction fields)
proposed_levers.config_keys: endpoint_floor_steps / wq_endpoint_recovery_steps stay as CEILINGS; add policy fields endpoint_floor_min_fraction, endpoint_floor_patience_fraction (frozen TuningPolicy, not user config)
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: ~2,300s on this 26-run group (16% of 14,676s): the 9 zero/noise-gain burns (2,797s measured) stop at ~min_steps+patience instead of full budget (t0_21 714->~330s, t01_05 692->~330s, t01_07 516->~190s, t01_02 462->~330s). Accuracy-neutral BY LEDGER: those cells' exit==entry (or < 1xSE) after the FULL burn, so an earlier keep-best stop returns the identical state; productive cells (t01_09 +4.1pp etc.) keep extending their horizon while improving. Certify late-cosine-gain risk with one A/B tier-0.1 wave.
proposed_levers.generic: Pure step-count geometry (fractions of whatever budget the recipe funds); no dataset/vehicle/S constants; applies identically to every mode's endpoint and to the legacy stabilize; tier-1/2 budgets grow => absolute savings grow.
proposed_levers.risk: Late cosine-phase gains after a long stagnation would be forfeited; bounded by keep-best (never a regression), needs one A/B wave to certify the patience fraction.

proposed_levers.lever: Crater guard on the floor arm (reachability-conditioned arming)
proposed_levers.mechanism: entry_gap_armed currently arms on gap >= 1x accuracy_se (endpoint_recovery.py:90-92), so conversion-CRATER cells arm the full ledger-funded burn. Gate the armed path on conditioning: arm only if entry >= pipeline_hard_floor (already computed per run, printed in every BUDGET line) - matching the stated [5u generalized] intent that the floor is for cells 'below the acceptance bar, not below by a conversion crater' (conversion_policy.py:36-38). Craters fall back to the default patience geometry (210-step stop). Also run the WQ retention pre-check BEFORE funding the endpoint: t0_17's entry 0.6226 was already 0.17 below the 0.795 retention bar, yet the full 2000-step burn ran first (log line 2334) and the run failed anyway.
proposed_levers.code_seams: src/mimarsinan/tuning/orchestration/frontier/endpoint_recovery.py:90-92,104; retention gate ordering in src/mimarsinan/tuning/tuners/normalization_aware_perceptron_quantization_tuner.py:84-86 (run_endpoint_recovery call site)
proposed_levers.config_keys: none new - reuses pipeline_hard_floor and accuracy_se already in TuningBudget
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: ~1,030s subset of lever-1 (t01_11 309.1 + t0_19 315.3 + t01_14 159.1 + t0_17 ~250) plus t0_17 fails ~250s earlier (fail-fast). Accuracy-neutral by ledger: EVERY armed cell with entry < 0.85 gained exactly +0.0000 over the full burn (0.6226, 0.7667, 0.7698, 0.8332), every cell that gained >= SE had entry >= 0.85; the guard is expressed via the existing hard-floor/SE machinery, not a constant.
proposed_levers.generic: Threshold = the run's own pipeline_hard_floor (derived from degradation_tolerance and the run's envelope), scale- and vehicle-free; craters are exactly the cells the [5u] rationale already excludes.
proposed_levers.risk: A crater cell that WOULD have recovered loses the attempt; the measured group shows zero such cells (all crater burns gained 0.0000).

proposed_levers.mechanism: Per 42-step check window the stage trains 1344 samples but evals 2048 through the same armed O(S)/genuine forward (check_interval=42, progress_eval_batches=16x128; BUDGET lines), i.e. eval ~25-35% of armed-stage wall. Double the endpoint check_interval (or set it so eval wall <= ~15% of stage wall - an S/depth-invariant rule since eval and train share the same forward) and keep patience in CHECK units so the stagnation window widens in steps (strictly more conservative). Keep the 4x confirm read (basic_trainer_steps.py:157-171) only on candidate improvements >= min_improvement.
proposed_levers.config_keys: none user-facing; a TuningPolicy endpoint check-interval multiplier
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: ~700-900s on the post-lever-1 group (20-25% of the surviving ~3,700s of productive armed burn; e.g. t01_09 WQ 464->~370s). Accuracy-neutral: keep-best is monotone (staleness bounded by one wider window); stop decisions in check units become more conservative, never less; the SE that all improvement thresholds key on (tuning_budget.py:32-35) is unchanged.
proposed_levers.generic: Cadence is derived from the budget's own check_interval and the eval/train cost ratio, no dataset constants; benefit scales with S and depth because eval shares the armed forward.
proposed_levers.risk: Keep-best granularity coarsens by one window (~84 steps of progress worst-case) - bounded and recoverable.

proposed_levers.mechanism: LavaSegmentMixin._run_neural_segment_scheduled runs all cores SERIALLY in topo order (segment_runner.py:97), each paying ~0.85s of Lava runtime spin-up, N=1 sample: t0_03 = 316 cores -> 428.8s, t01_19 -> 311.9s. The topo order is sorted by core latency (segment_runner.py:82-84); cores within the same latency level depend only on already-computed upstream buffers, so execute each latency wave in a process pool (nevresim already runs 25 simulator processes in the sibling step).
proposed_levers.code_seams: src/mimarsinan/chip_simulation/lava_loihi/segment_runner.py:82-97 (topo loop); src/mimarsinan/chip_simulation/lava_loihi/core_lava.py (_run_core_lava, the per-core unit of work)
proposed_levers.config_keys: optional worker-count env via common/env.py; default = os.cpu_count-bounded
proposed_levers.predicted_delta: ~790s group-wide (890s Loihi total -> ~100s at 8-16 workers): t0_03 832->~470s, t01_19 657->~390s. Accuracy-neutral BY CONSTRUCTION: per-core sims are independent given their input buffers, outputs bit-identical and order-independently aggregated (parity gates unchanged - all Loihi PROFILE lines already show delta=+0.0000).
proposed_levers.generic: Applies to any vehicle/mode Lava supports; tier-1/2 models have MORE cores, so serial wall grows linearly without it - the lever's value scales with model size.
proposed_levers.risk: Lava runtime process-safety (spawn workers, no shared runtime state); memory per concurrent worker.

proposed_levers.lever: Parity-gated S-invariant train forward for endpoint steps
proposed_levers.mechanism: The armed forward costs O(S) (LIF S=32: 7.0 steps/s vs 43.4 analytic). For conversions whose train-surrogate is bit-exact to the armed forward (is_bit_parity_lossless_conversion predicate; and LIF whose staircase equivalence is bit-exact per the LIF<->staircase parity results), run the 16k/2k endpoint TRAINING steps on the S-invariant surrogate while keeping every keep-best/gate/confirm READ on the armed deployed forward. Gate per-run with a pre-flight bit-exactness probe (surrogate vs armed outputs on a probe batch); any mismatch -> fall back to the armed path unchanged.

(4) Entry-anchored keep-best (basic_trainer_steps.py:141-144 [M-guard]) makes the stage non-destructive — the low-risk claim holds. OVERSTATEMENT: "the machinery already exists — endpoint_recovery simply never passes it" is wrong for the failing arm. The armed (ledger-funded, 16k) floor sets min_steps=budget (endpoint_recovery.py:116, patience deliberately disarmed), and the plateau branch only fires when step_idx+1 >= min_s AND stale_checks >= patience (basic_trainer_steps.py:179) — so passing the existing plateau kwargs would be a NO-OP on the 16k floor. Also existing plateau semantics differ from the proposed rescue: _rebuild_scheduler_at_reduced_lr drops to CONSTANT LR with no restore-best and no warmup. The dead-run predicate + restore-best + warmup restart is genuinely NEW code (the lever's seam list implicitly admits this).

MEASUREMENTS — outcome-level claims VERIFIED; trace-level claims unverifiable; one counter-observation. Verified: fal_t0_21 and fal_t01_24 configs are byte-identical except experiment_name (diff of _RUN_CONFIG/config.json), hcm 0.9316 vs 0.9671 = +3.55pp — matches "+3.5pp identical-config sibling" exactly (generated/_campaign/results/). The two cells the lever names as collapsed (t01_05 0.9104, t0_21 0.9316) are exactly the two low outliers of the 7-cell mixer family (others 0.9582-0.9694), and t0_21's 0.9316 matches the entry-restore signature (floor entries ~0.93) — consistent with a dead floor exiting at entry. check_interval=42 makes "<=84 steps" = 2 checks, internally consistent. UNVERIFIABLE: the specific per-check traces ("t01_05 first check 0.098; t0_21 0.9205 then 0.10; dead 16k steps") are not in this repo — the July-9 campaign's per-check traces are absent (fal_* run-dir GUI states are stale July-8 leftovers; _fixall_local logs belong to a different July-8 wave where t01_05's floor was HEALTHY, 0.9314→0.9582, HCM 0.9583 vs campaign 0.9104). Not refuted, but the trace citations rest on data I could not locate. COUNTER-OBSERVATION: the one floor-collapse trace that IS observable (July-8 fal_t01_24 WQ live metrics: 0.9255→0.198 within ~4 checks at lr 2e-3) SELF-RECOVERED to ~0.92 under existing cosine decay + keep-best — collapse is real but not always terminal, and that collapse happened AFTER early checks beat entry, a pattern the proposed predicate ("best never beat entry+SE") would MISS. The predicate covers only the never-took-off pattern; it should also arm on post-progress craters (current < hard_floor for M consecutive checks). Consequently treat +3.5pp as a best-case bound: identical-config family outcomes form a spread (0.9316/0.9436/0.9526/0.9671 across waves), not clean bimodality, and rescue efficacy from the restored-best state is unmeasured (though restoring the LIVE best state before retraining is exactly the proven revive-then-FT pattern from artifact 50 — gradient-from-dead-state fails, revive works — so the design aligns with, rather than contradicts, prior verdicts).

GENERICITY — HOLDS structurally. Guard inputs (entry, accuracy_se, pipeline_hard_floor) are all run-derived; backoff is multiplicative; no MNIST/mixer constants smuggled. On tier-1 deep_cnn floor stages in the same waves (t0_03, t01_15, t01_16, t01_19) entries were 0.97-0.99 with no collapse — the guard stays dormant there, so "zero change on healthy floors" is structurally sound; but note the fragility EVIDENCE is so far mixer/sync-specific, so tier-1/2 benefit is extrapolation, not measurement.

CONFLICTS — NONE found. No existing divergence guard in src (no duplication). The [5u] probe-validated arm (lr 2e-3, cosine, patience disarmed) is untouched unless the guard fires. "High-S warmup stages hurt" (artifact 51) concerns the S-curriculum, not LR warmup; the STE fast-path integration plan explicitly recommends warmup+cosine; "more budget HURTS" is about the fire-once surrogate ladder, not the endpoint floor. One caution: recovery_lr_plateau failed to graduate default-on in the earlier per-cycle-recovery ablation (kept opt-in) — different seam, not a refutation, but the new wiring must be a separate default-off flag with its own ablation per repo discipline, and TuningPolicy is the frozen never-user-configured SSOT, so enabling means flipping a policy default with measured evidence.

verifications.reasoning:
The cited seams exist and the O(S) cost mechanism is real, but the lever's load-bearing parity claim is mis-scoped, and its predicted delta collapses under its own gate.

1. PREDICATE SCOPE MISMATCH (vacuous where gated, ungated where profitable). `is_bit_parity_lossless_conversion` (src/mimarsinan/chip_simulation/spiking_semantics.py:73-82) covers ONLY analytical ttfs; its docstring explicitly excludes lif: "The near-lossless modes (lif, ttfs_quantized, synchronized) keep a real endpoint gap". By the predicate's own definition ("trained forward is per-neuron bit-equivalent to its deployed executor"), the covered family ALREADY trains on the S-invariant closed-form — there is no O(S) armed cost to remove, so the predicate-gated portion of the lever saves ~0s. Meanwhile both headline savings cells are LIF: test_configs/tier0/t0_04_lif_deepmlp_d8_wq_s32.json and test_configs/tier0_1/t01_02_lif_mmixcore_wq_s16.json — outside the predicate, carried only by the ad-hoc "LIF<->staircase parity" extension.

2. THE CITED PARITY RESULT DOES NOT COVER THE ARMED FORWARD. The measured "LIF≡staircase bit-exact" result (mimarsinan_research/docs/research_artifacts_for_cascaded_ttfs_tuning/50_localization_correctable_gain.md, Finding 1, lines 16-26) compares LIF-RATE nodes vs the TTFS analytical staircase — two S-cheap surrogates, equivalent to EACH OTHER, on an isolated digits harness. It says nothing about either being bit-exact to the armed cycle-accurate forward that endpoint training actually pays for (conversion_policy.py:50-53: "each armed step pays the O(S) cycle-accurate LIF forward"). Structurally they cannot be equivalent in general: the armed path propagates genuine temporal spike trains between layers (spike_trains.py `lif_spike_train`; SpikingHybridCoreFlow per-cycle path), and with signed membrane integration spike counts are order-dependent, so constant-rate drive diverges from temporal trains at network level. The repo's own SSOT states this is exactly why the flag exists: config_schema/registry/entries_conversion.py describes cycle_accurate_lif_forward as "the correctness mechanism that keeps the QAT train-forward bit-exact to the deployed eval-forward. The LIF recipe always folds it ON; it is never a knob." If the rate surrogate were bit-exact to the cycle forward, that mechanism would be pointless O(S) waste. Novena firing (t0_02) provably breaks equivalence (firing_strategy.py:83-88 hard-requires cycle-accurate for zero-reset).

3. PREDICTED DELTA INCONSISTENT WITH ITS OWN GATE. The steps/s arithmetic is internally consistent (~2000 steps at 7.0/s ≈ 287s vs 43.4/s ≈ 46-65s, matching t0_04), but the ~600-700s claim is dominated by LIF cells whose pre-flight bit-exactness probe will fail (per point 2) and — per the lever's own fallback rule — leave the armed path unchanged. Realizable savings ≈ 0.

4. GENERICITY: the self-disqualifying probe is honestly designed and not MNIST-smuggling, but it inverts the payoff claim: on tier1/2 (deep_cnn d8+, S=32-64) parity failure is MORE certain (deeper temporal interleaving, cupy-vs-torch backend fp32 numerics), so the lever no-ops precisely where "grows with S" predicts the largest wins. Also the LIF rate surrogate is not truly S-invariant — `_spikes_and_scale` expands to (T,...) multi-step, O(T) fused — a mischaracterization.

5. CONFLICTS WITH EXISTING INVARIANTS AND VERDICTS: (a) cycle_accurate_lif_forward is in `_RECIPE_OWNED_CORRECTNESS_KEYS` (config_schema/deployment_derivation.py:42) and documented "never a knob" (defaults.py:50-52), yet the lever names it "the flag this conditions"; (b) the established parity verdict that tuners must keep the genuine spike forward installed "through commit+recovery+downstream like LIF; else SCM gate trips"; (c) family precedents against surrogate-trained endpoints: staircase warm-start poisons the cascaded basin (genuine deploy = chance), and the STE clean-loop equivalence did not transfer to the controller path (0.82 cap). Even under exact forward parity, "neutral by construction" is overstated: the two paths' surrogate BACKWARDS (fused multi-step cupy ATan vs single-step torch loop) differ numerically, so the 2000-step trajectory diverges; keep-best armed reads bound but do not eliminate this.

What survives: the abstract cost observation is correct, but the only defensible reformulations are different levers (reduce armed-read frequency, exploit the WQ ladder's projection-only rungs, or fund a measured — not bit-exact — surrogate A/B per mode with deployed-read gates), not this parity-gated forward swap.

verifications.reasoning:
The DIAGNOSIS is measured-confirmed, but the lever's mechanism is broken at both cited seams; as specified it delivers ~0s from part (a) and part (b) is a literal no-op.

DIAGNOSIS CONFIRMED: generated/_campaign/results shows t0_08 Simulation=181.06s (lever: 181.1s) and t01_17=184.47s; an independent local log (generated/_fixall_local_slowhead/t0_08_...log) shows the same wall structure — Simulation 106.72s with segment sims only 47.2s, i.e. >50% is emit/compile/IO overhead. t01_06 AQ=148.32s measured (lever: 136.7s, close), and src/mimarsinan/tuning/tuners/activation_quantization_tuner.py:68-73 confirms non-sync modes keep the legacy 4x max_training_steps stabilize (min_steps=budget in smooth_adaptation_run.py:109 forces the full burn).

REFUTATION OF (a): (1) The proposed cache key — "ir_graph build_token" — is uuid.uuid4().hex generated per construction (src/mimarsinan/mapping/ir/graph.py:12-13,33), NOT content-derived; every run mints a new token, so cross-run hits are impossible, and within a run each segment already compiles exactly once in a parallel pool (simulation_runner/hybrid.py:_prepare_all_segments). The lever misreads the token's role: hybrid_mapping uses it for STALENESS detection inside one run, not content-addressed reuse. As designed the cache saves ~0s. (2) A correct content-keyed cache ALREADY EXISTS and is dormant: src/mimarsinan/chip_simulation/nevresim/compile_cache.py (mapping_connectivity_hash + policy_hash covering S/latency/mode/thresholding/types — the exact invalidation set the lever lists as an open risk) is wired into NevresimDriver.emit_main_and_compile but no production caller passes compile_cache_dir (only profiling). The real work is plumbing, not building. (3) "Accuracy-neutral: same binary, byte-identical sim" is FALSE today: the runtime main template (code_generation/main_cpp_template_runtime.py) bakes ABSOLUTE paths to chip_spans.txt, chip_weights.txt and the inputs dir of the compile-time working directory; campaign waves use distinct run dirs (fal_/x2e_/_wcal_ prefixes), so a cross-run reused binary silently loads the OLD wave's weights and inputs — wrong results, not a byte-identical sim. Relocatability (paths via argv) is a required redesign the lever omits. (4) Concurrency: campaign cells run in parallel; compile_nevresim.py:112 already documents the shared-binary ETXTBSY/wrong-simulator hazard that a naive shared cache dir with shutil.copy2 store reintroduces. (5) Delta accounting: only the compile is cacheable — weight emit and input file IO are per-run by construction (weights load at runtime), so "150-250s group-wide" over-attributes; and for tier1/2 CIFAR-class deep_cnn the sim term grows while compile stays fixed, shrinking the relative win (the absolute win would survive — the cache idea itself is generic, just not this design).

REFUTATION OF (b): adding endpoint_recovery_steps to _TTFS_QUANTIZED_RECIPE_KNOBS is a NO-OP. Both the stabilize-zeroing (_stabilization_budget, activation_quantization_tuner.py:68-73) and the endpoint hook (_post_stabilization_hook, :75-91) are gated on sync_exact_qat_active(config), which is hard-false for ttfs_quantized (adaptation_manager.py:46-55 requires the resolved mode to be synchronized ttfs_cycle). The knob is read only inside that gated hook. The migration therefore needs a tuner-code change not covered by the lever's code_seams. Further, "already-proven replacement" overreaches: sync's endpoint proof (T6) is coupled to sync_exact_qat — the endpoint trains the EXACT deployed composition (ceil kernel + grid snap); ttfsq's AQ trains the floor+half-step PROXY, and the _TTFS_QUANTIZED_RATIONALE in conversion_policy.py:117-122 deliberately keeps ttfsq "on the floor+half-step proxy; the exact-kernel endpoint promotion is an X4 follow-up (mbh_t6_sync_exact_kernel)" — the lever half-conflicts with a stated policy deferral. Delta is also overstated: measured ttfsq AQ walls are 148.3s (t01_06), 50.4s (t0_11), <20s (t0_12/t0_13) — "~100s on ttfsq cells" is really one cell, and the replacement endpoint itself trains 600+freed steps, so net < the stabilize wall.
